bodyPreview: Rendered marketplace detail page confirms Genome Explorer v3 title, Sequencing.com provider, free price, exact detail route, and utility columns/context for genome search and browsing. The page exposes rendered DOM/meta content rather than rich pageProps.reportData or a sample report file.
evidenceNotes: Marketplace detail page was opened in the in-app browser and resolved to the exact report URL.

This detail page exposes rendered DOM/meta content rather than the richer pageProps.reportData shape; use it for exact route availability, title, provider/price metadata, and detail-page boundary evidence only.
Use overview/marketing text only for report purpose and marketplace positioning, not for local genetic, medical, trait, medication, ancestry, fitness, or performance conclusions.
No raw genome data, uploaded genome data, or account navigation text is included in this artifact.

meta.ogDescriptionPreview: Free genome search engine and DNA data viewer. Genome Explorer lets you search, browse, and explore your genetic data. Works with any DNA test file.

source: in-app browser sequencing.com marketplace detail page rendered DOM/meta extraction
summaryPreview: Discover, learn, and explore your deep DNA data. Genome Explorer is presented as an online browser and search engine for genome data.
title: Genome Explorer v3
visibleParagraphs: Discover, learn, and explore your deep DNA data. Genome Explorer is the world's first online browser and search engine for your genome.
Compatible with data aligned to any reference genome, including GRCh38, hg19/GRCh37 and hg18/GRCh36.
Genome Explorer can search by RCV ID, chromosome, position, variant ID, gene, variant type, status, condition, classification, and confidence when those search results are supplied.
The page describes SNP/SNV, INDEL, CNV, SV, and mitochondrial heteroplasmy compatibility, but no user genome output or sample report rows were exposed during capture.

Each row in Genome Explorer provides information on a position within your genome, with columns such as RCV ID, chromosome, position, variant ID, gene, impact, variant type, reference, alternate allele, user data, condition, status, classification, confidence, references, and links.
